_comment: Single source of truth for pre-tool-use.js hook regression tests. Both test_pre_tool_use_hook.py and test_pre_tool_use_hook.js iterate this spec — update HERE first, then tests stay in sync automatically. Each entry: name (unique id), class (bypass class 1-19 per .claude/hooks/README.md, 0 = baseline), branch (initial repo branch; null = skip git setup, hook runs in a non-git temp dir), command (Bash payload command) or tool_name+tool_input (non-Bash payload) or fixture (filename under tests/fixtures/), expected_exit (0 = allow, 2 = block), check_stderr (case-insensitive substring required in stderr; null = no message check), description (human-readable). Classes 9-15 close the seven CRIT-008 bypass classes documented in issue #102; classes 16-17 close the two residual bypasses found at the test gate (c-not-last nested-shell clusters and the `sudo` wrapper); classes 18-19 close two wrapper idioms found at the merge-family test gate (`eval '<payload>'` and the backslash-escaped `\git` command token) that bypass the commit-producing detection — agent-layer defense-in-depth for the git-layer pre-merge-commit boundary.

tests.name: refspec_to_protected_blocks
tests.description: Class 2: refspec-to-protected blocks regardless of HEAD

tests.name: implicit_push_from_protected_blocks
tests.description: Class 1: `git push` with no refspec while HEAD is protected

tests.name: broad_push_all_blocks
tests.description: Class 4: `--all` blocks regardless of HEAD

tests.name: broad_push_mirror_blocks
tests.description: Class 4: `--mirror` blocks regardless of HEAD

tests.name: commit_on_protected_blocks
tests.description: Class 5: `git commit` while HEAD is protected

tests.name: merge_on_protected_blocks
tests.description: Class 6: commit-producing subcommand on protected HEAD

tests.name: rebase_on_protected_blocks
tests.description: Class 6: rebase variant of commit-producing subcommand

tests.name: nested_shell_blocks
tests.description: Class 7: `bash -c "git push ..."` is re-evaluated

tests.name: chained_subcommand_blocks
tests.description: Class 8: `&&`-chained git push to protected
tests.command: true && git push origin feat/x:{{ cookiecutter.default_branch_name }}

tests.name: feature_branch_push_allows
tests.description: Baseline: pushing a feature branch to itself is fine

tests.name: feature_branch_commit_allows
tests.description: Baseline: committing on a feature branch is fine

tests.name: non_bash_tool_passes_through
tests.description: Baseline: non-Bash tool calls are out of scope

tests.name: non_git_bash_passes_through
tests.description: Baseline: non-git Bash commands pass through

tests.name: empty_command_passes_through
tests.description: Baseline: empty Bash command is a no-op

tests.name: push_head_on_protected_blocks
tests.description: Class 9: `git push origin HEAD` while HEAD is protected — HEAD refspec normalized to current branch

tests.name: push_head_on_feature_allows
tests.description: Class 9 guard: `git push origin HEAD` from a feature branch stays allowed (no false positive)

tests.name: single_ampersand_chain_blocks
tests.description: Class 10: single `&` job-control separator still splits into subcommands

tests.name: nested_shell_combined_flags_blocks
tests.description: Class 11: `bash -lc "git push ..."` combined short-flag nested shell is re-evaluated

tests.name: nested_shell_semicolon_payload_blocks
tests.description: Class 12: quoted nested-shell payload with an inner `;` stays intact until re-evaluated

tests.name: wrapper_env_git_push_blocks
tests.description: Class 13: `env` wrapper around git push is peeled before the git check

tests.name: wrapper_command_git_push_blocks
tests.description: Class 13: `command` wrapper around git push is peeled before the git check

tests.name: wrapper_nice_git_push_blocks
tests.description: Class 13: `nice` wrapper around git push is peeled before the git check

tests.name: wrapper_xargs_git_push_blocks
tests.description: Class 13: `xargs` wrapper around git push is peeled before the git check

tests.name: broad_push_branches_blocks
tests.description: Class 14: `--branches` broad push mode blocks like `--all`

tests.name: commit_fail_closed_on_lookup_failure
tests.description: Class 15: `git commit` with a failed current-branch lookup fails closed (non-git dir)

tests.name: push_fail_closed_on_lookup_failure
tests.description: Class 15: `git push` with a failed current-branch lookup fails closed (non-git dir)

tests.name: non_git_in_non_git_dir_allows
tests.description: Class 15 guard: fail-closed is scoped to git commands — a plain `ls` in a non-git dir stays allowed

tests.name: nested_shell_c_not_last_bash_cl_blocks
tests.description: Class 16: `bash -cl "git push ..."` — combined short-flag cluster where `c` is NOT the last letter is still recognized as a `-c` nested shell

tests.name: nested_shell_c_not_last_sh_ci_blocks
tests.description: Class 16: `sh -ci "git push ..."` — `c`-not-last cluster on sh is recognized as a `-c` nested shell

tests.name: nested_shell_c_not_last_feature_allows
tests.description: Class 16 guard: `bash -cl "git push origin feat/x"` to a feature branch stays allowed (no false positive on the widened cluster pattern)

tests.name: wrapper_sudo_git_push_blocks
tests.description: Class 17: `sudo git push origin <protected>` — sudo is peeled as a trivial wrapper before the git check

tests.name: wrapper_sudo_flag_arg_git_push_blocks
tests.description: Class 17: `sudo -u ci git push origin <protected>` — sudo's `-u <user>` arg-taking option is consumed so the wrapper still peels to git
tests.command: sudo -u ci git push origin {{ cookiecutter.default_branch_name }}

tests.name: wrapper_sudo_non_git_allows
tests.description: Class 17 guard: `sudo systemctl restart nginx` is a non-git target — wrapper peeling must not over-block it

tests.name: eval_cherry_pick_on_protected_blocks
tests.description: Class 18: `eval 'git cherry-pick x'` while HEAD is protected — eval's quoted payload is extracted and recursively evaluated so the commit-producing subcommand is caught

tests.name: eval_merge_on_protected_blocks
tests.description: Class 18: `eval "git merge feat/x"` while HEAD is protected — double-quoted eval payload is likewise extracted and re-evaluated

tests.name: eval_git_status_on_protected_allows
tests.description: Class 18 guard: `eval 'git status'` on protected is read-only — extracting and re-evaluating the payload must not over-block a non-mutating git command

tests.name: eval_non_git_allows
tests.description: Class 18 guard: `eval 'npm run build'` is a non-git payload — eval peeling must not over-block it

tests.name: backslash_git_merge_on_protected_blocks
tests.description: Class 19: `\git merge feat` while HEAD is protected — a single leading backslash on the command token is stripped before the git check

tests.name: backslash_git_status_on_feature_allows
tests.description: Class 19 guard: `\git status` on a feature branch stays allowed — backslash normalization must not over-block a non-mutating command off a protected branch
